content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 23, 2017

Philip C. Kohn
City Attorney
City of Laguna Beach
611 Anton Blvd., Suite 1400
Costa Mesa, CA 92626

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-17-046

Dear Mr. Kohn:

This letter responds to your request for informal assistance on behalf of Laguna Beach City
Councilmember Steve Dicterow regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").²

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of
interest or Section 1090.

QUESTION

May Councilmember Dicterow participate in decisions involving the City's potential
acquisition of a privately-owned lot given that he had past discussions with the property's
leaseholder about acquiring an interest in the property for personal investment purposes?

CONCLUSION

Yes. The Act does not prohibit Councilmember Dicterow from participating in decisions
regarding the property because he has no financial interest in the decisions.

_______________

¹ Because your request is general in nature and you do not provide specifics about the property at issue or the
parties involved, we treat your request as one for informal assistance. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor
with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

² The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. I-17-046
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are the City Attorney for the City of Laguna Beach ("City"). In the early part of 2016, the City Council authorized the City Manager to contact the owner of a privately-held lot that was being used for commercial purposes (the "property") and to determine whether the property owner would be interested in selling the property to the City for public uses.

A short time afterward, Councilmember Dicterow was approached by the holder of a leasehold in the property (the "lessee"), inquiring whether he had any interest in acquiring the leasehold or possibly a sublease.

Councilmember Dicterow therafter recused himself from all subsequent City Council discussions and deliberations concerning the property. However, before any substantive discussions or negotiations took place between Councilmember Dicterow and the lessee, the lessee informed the Councilmember that he was no longer interested in transferring or assigning any interest in the property.

The City Council decided in mid-2016 not to further explore acquisition of the property. There have not been any discussions between the Councilmember and the lessee regarding the leasehold, and the Councilmember has no current or future intent to engage in any such discussions.

Recently, some members of the City Council have expressed a renewed interest in inviting the owner of the property to engage in negotiations for a possible sale of the lot to the City.

You seek formal advice on behalf of Councilmember Dicterow regarding whether he may participate in future City Council discussions and deliberations regarding the property, and possible negotiations about the potential acquisition by the City of the property.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions only apply to conflicts of interests based on the following:

• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).)

• Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b).)

• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)

File No. 1-17-046
Page No. 3

• Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).)

• Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).)

• In addition, a public official's personal finances are deemed to be directly involved in a governmental decision that will have any financial effect on his or her personal finances or those of his or her immediate family. (Section 87103.)

Based on the facts provided, it does not appear Councilmember Dicterow has any financial interests that would be implicated by the City's decision to consider, discuss, or possibly negotiate the potential acquisition of the privately-owned lot. Assuming the facts presented are accurate, he will not have an interest in the decisions at issue.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Emelyn C. Rodriguez
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ECR:jgl

leaseholder about acquiring an interest in the property for personal investment purposes?

the City Council authorized the City Manager to contact the owner of a privately-held lot that was

A short time afterward, Council member Dicterow was approached by the holder of a 
leasehold in the property (the "lessee"), inquiring whether he had any interest in acquiring the 
leasehold or possibly a sublease. 
Councilmember Dicterow thereafter recused himself from all subsequent City Council 
discussions and deliberations concerning the property. However, before any substantive discussions 
or negotiations took place between Councilmember Dicterow and the lessee, the lessee informed 
the Councilmember that he was no longer interested in transferring or assigning any interest in the

The City Council decided in mid-2016 not to further explore acquisition of the property. 
There have not been any discussions between the Councilmember and the lessee regarding the

You seek formal advice on behalf of Councilmember Dicterow regarding whether he may

sections.analysis:
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions only apply to conflicts of 
interests based on the following: 
• 
Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).) 
• 
Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 
or more. (Section 87103(b).) 
• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)  
• Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
• Any donor of, or any intennediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or 
more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months 
prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).) 
• 
In addition, a public official's personal finances are deemed to be directly involved in a 
governmental decision that will have any financial effect on his or her personal finances or 
those of his or her immediate family. (Section 87103.) 
Based on the facts provided, it does not appear Councilmember Dicterow has any financial 
interests that would be implicated by the City's decision to consider, discuss, or possibly negotiate 
the potential acquisition of the privately-owned lot. Assuming the facts presented are accurate, he 
will not have an interest in the decisions at issue.
